question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI report has already been released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics, showing a 0.3% monthly increase. This is significantly below the 0.8% threshold needed for YES resolution. The market should resolve NO with 100% certainty, yet is still pricing at 68% YES, creating a massive arbitrage opportunity.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased only 0.3% monthly
Market requires >0.8% increase for YES resolution
Report already released on April 10, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET
Market closes April 10, 2026 at 12:25 PM ET - same day as data release
risks: Potential data revision (though CPI revisions are typically minor)
Possible misunderstanding of market resolution criteria
Technical issues preventing market price from updating to reflect known outcome
